steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage immediately explains “mingled” through a simile: “Like the blending of an exotic dream plant / With the fragrant perfume of a strange, frail flower.” The word “blending” serves as the poem's own definition of what “mingled” means.

steps.title: Match the target meaning
steps.content: The target meaning is to blend or combine separate things together. Combined (B) directly expresses this—when elements are mingled, they unite or merge. The other choices describe unrelated concepts and don't capture this sense of merging. That matches choice B — B is correct.
